devanagari: सांख्यक्रियायोग वर्णनम् । श्रीउद्धव उवाच । क्रियायोगं समाचक्ष्व भवदाराधनं प्रभो । यस्मात्त्वां ये यथार्चन्ति सात्वताः सात्वतर्षभ ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: sāṃkhyakriyāyoga varṇanamśrīuddhava uvācakriyāyogaṃ samācakṣva bhavadārādhanaṃ prabhoyasmāttvāṃ ye yathārcanti sātvatāḥ sātvatarṣabha
translation:
Uddhava said:
Be pleased to explain to me the Yoga of active service (Kriyā-Yoga) consisting of the propitiation of your worship, O Lord I What type of votaries worship you? With what motive do they propitiate you? What is the procedure of their worship, O Lord of Sāttvatas.

devanagari: एतद् वदन्ति मुनयो मुहुर्निःश्रेयसं नृणाम् । नारदो भगवान् व्यास आचार्योऽग्गिरसः सुतः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: etad vadanti munayo muhurniḥśreyasaṃ nṛṇāmnārado bhagavān vyāsa ācāryo'ggirasaḥ sutaḥ
translation: Sages like Nārada, the worshipful Vyāsa and Bṛhaspati, the son of Aṅgiras, the preceptor (of gods), have repeatedly asserted that this course is conducive to the Final Beatitude to men.

devanagari: निःसृतं ते मुखाम्भोजाद् यदाह भगवानजः । पुत्रेभ्यो भृगुमुख्येभ्यो देव्यै च भगवान् भवः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: niḥsṛtaṃ te mukhāmbhojād yadāha bhagavānajaḥputrebhyo bhṛgumukhyebhyo devyai ca bhagavān bhavaḥ
translation: The teaching that was issued from your lotus-mouth was the same which the glorious unborn god Brahmā communicated to his sons of whom Bhṛgu was the chief, and also what god Śiva imparted to his divine consort Pārvatī,

devanagari: एतद् वै सर्ववर्णानां आश्रमाणां च सम्मतम् । श्रेयसामुत्तमं मन्ये स्त्रीशूद्राणां च मानद ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: etad vai sarvavarṇānāṃ āśramāṇāṃ ca sammatamśreyasāmuttamaṃ manye strīśūdrāṇāṃ ca mānada
translation: That teaching is approved as meritorious to persons belonging to all castes (Varṇas) and stages in life (Āśramas) I believe it is the best course conducive to emancipation from Saṃsāra even for women and Śūdras.

devanagari: एतत्कमलपत्राक्ष कर्मबन्धविमोचनम् । भक्ताय चानुरक्ताय ब्रूहि विश्वेश्वरेश्वर ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: etatkamalapatrākṣa karmabandhavimocanambhaktāya cānuraktāya brūhi viśveśvareśvara
translation: O Lord with eyes like lotus-petals! Be pleased to elucidate that course which unties the bondage of Karma (permanently) to me who am your beloved devotee, O Lord of the masters of this universe (the Trinity of gods—Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra).

devanagari: श्रीभगवानुवाच । न ह्यन्तोऽनन्तपारस्य कर्मकाण्डस्य चोद्धव । सङ्क्षिप्तं वर्णयिष्यामि यथावदनुपूर्वशः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībhagavānuvācana hyanto'nantapārasya karmakāṇḍasya coddhavasaṅkṣiptaṃ varṇayiṣyāmi yathāvadanupūrvaśaḥ
translation:
The Lord replied:
Limitless is the Karma-kāṇḍa (as courses and practices of ritualistic worship of the Lord are infinite). I shall, however, describe that to you succinctly and in proper sequence.

devanagari: वैदिकस्तान्त्रिको मिश्र इति मे त्रिविधो मखः । त्रयाणामीप्सितेनैव विधिना मां समर्चरेत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: vaidikastāntriko miśra iti me trividho makhaḥtrayāṇāmīpsitenaiva vidhinā māṃ samarcaret
translation: My worship is threefold—Vedic, Tantric and a synthesis of these two. But of these three modes, one should offer me worship according to the method of his choice (and suitability).

devanagari: यदा स्वनिगमेनोक्तं द्विजत्वं प्राप्य पूरुषः । यथा यजेत मां भक्त्या श्रद्धया तन्निबोध मे ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā svanigamenoktaṃ dvijatvaṃ prāpya pūruṣaḥyathā yajeta māṃ bhaktyā śraddhayā tannibodha me
translation: Learn from me the method how a person, having attained the status of a twice-born when he is invested with a sacred thread according to the injunctions laid down in the Vedas, should worship me, with faith and devotion.

devanagari: अर्चायां स्थण्डिलेऽग्नौ वा सूर्ये वाप्सु हृदि द्विजः । द्रव्येण भक्तियुक्तोऽर्चेत् स्वगुरुं माममायया ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: arcāyāṃ sthaṇḍile'gnau vā sūrye vāpsu hṛdi dvijaḥdravyeṇa bhaktiyukto'rcet svaguruṃ māmamāyayā
translation: With sincere devotion and without any fraudulent motive (of expecting some fruit or ulterior objective in return), a person should worship me, the preceptor (or the father of world) with the articles of worship (by offering these to me) through the medium of an image or an altar of a sacrifice or the fire or the Sun or the waters or (image enthroned in) his heart or of a Brāhmaṇa.

devanagari: पूर्वं स्नानं प्रकुर्वीत धौतदन्तोऽङ्गशुद्धये । उभयैरपि च स्नानं मन्त्रैर्मृद् ग्रहणादिना ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: pūrvaṃ snānaṃ prakurvīta dhautadanto'ṅgaśuddhayeubhayairapi ca snānaṃ mantrairmṛd grahaṇādinā
translation: For bodily cleanliness, he should first brush his teeth and take his bath. While bathing he should use earth (and other materials) for washing, muttering all the while Vedic and Tāntric mantras (sacred formulae).

devanagari: सन्ध्योपास्त्यादिकर्माणि वेदेनाचोदितानि मे । पूजां तैः कल्पयेत् सम्यक् सङ्कल्पः कर्मपावनीम् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sandhyopāstyādikarmāṇi vedenācoditāni mepūjāṃ taiḥ kalpayet samyak saṅkalpaḥ karmapāvanīm
translation: After the performance of the Twilight prayer (Sandhyā, Brahmayajña, etc.) and other duties which have been enjoined by me through the Veda, he should, with a pure, solemn determination (of propitiating me) perform my worship which sanctifies (i.e. relieves one from the bondage of) karmas.

devanagari: शैली दारुमयी लौही लेप्या लेख्या च सैकती । मनोमयी मणिमयी प्रतिमाष्टविधा स्मृता ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: śailī dārumayī lauhī lepyā lekhyā ca saikatīmanomayī maṇimayī pratimāṣṭavidhā smṛtā
translation: An image is said to be of eight kinds: It may be made of stone or of wood or of metals (like gold, silver, etc.) or of earth or of sandal paste or may be a painting (on the wall) or of sand or a mental image or made of a precious stone.

devanagari: चलाचलेति द्विविधा प्रतिष्ठा जीवमन्दिरम् । उद्वासावाहने न स्तः स्थिरायामुद्धवार्चने ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: calācaleti dvividhā pratiṣṭhā jīvamandiramudvāsāvāhane na staḥ sthirāyāmuddhavārcane
translation: An image which is the shrine of the Lord, is of two kinds—movable (e.g. a Śālagrāma pebble) or immovable. While adoring an immovable image, āvāhana (invoking the presence of the deity) and udvāsa (or visarjana, a farewell to the deity) are not necessary, O Uddhava.

devanagari: अस्थिरायां विकल्पः स्यात् स्थण्डिले तु भवेद् द्वयम् । स्नपनं त्वविलेप्यायां अन्यत्र परिमार्जनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: asthirāyāṃ vikalpaḥ syāt sthaṇḍile tu bhaved dvayamsnapanaṃ tvavilepyāyāṃ anyatra parimārjanam
translation: In worshipping a movable image, one can exercise a choice whether invoking and evoking of the deity in the image should be done. But in the case of an altar of sacrifice both must be done. If the image be not of earth (or sandalpaste) or a painted picture, it may be bathed, but in the case of other material, it should be sprinkled with water.

devanagari: द्रव्यैः प्रसिद्धैर्मद्यागः प्रतिमादिष्वमायिनः । भक्तस्य च यथालब्धैः हृदि भावेन चैव हि ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dravyaiḥ prasiddhairmadyāgaḥ pratimādiṣvamāyinaḥbhaktasya ca yathālabdhaiḥ hṛdi bhāvena caiva hi
translation: My worship through the medium of images should be performed with excellent articles. But a sincere (guileless) devotee may worship me with whatever articles are available to him. In the case of worshipping me in the mind, it should be done with articles created by the mind with utmost devotion.

devanagari: स्नानालङ्करणं प्रेष्ठं अर्चायामेव तूद्धव । स्थण्डिले तत्त्वविन्यासो वह्नावाज्यप्लुतं हविः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: snānālaṅkaraṇaṃ preṣṭhaṃ arcāyāmeva tūddhavasthaṇḍile tattvavinyāso vahnāvājyaplutaṃ haviḥ
translation: Bathing and decorating an image pleases me the most, O Uddhava. In the case of an altar, invocation of the specific deities at their respective places by means of mantras is desirable (or placement of twenty-five Tattvas—categories or principles by uttering twenty-five letters from ka to ma). In the case of sacred fire, oblations with ghee be offered.

devanagari: सूर्ये चाभ्यर्हणं प्रेष्ठं सलिले सलिलादिभिः । श्रद्धयोपाहृतं प्रेष्ठं भक्तेन मम वार्यपि ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sūrye cābhyarhaṇaṃ preṣṭhaṃ salile salilādibhiḥśraddhayopāhṛtaṃ preṣṭhaṃ bhaktena mama vāryapi
translation: In the Sun-god, worship with Arghya (respectful offering of water and other articles of reception) and prayers is the most pleasing to me; and in water, with libations of water and flower, etc. Even if mere water is offered with faith and devotion by my votary, it gives me the highest pleasure.

devanagari: भूर्यप्यभक्तोपाहृतं न मे तोषाय कल्पते । गन्धो धूपः सुमनसो दीपोऽन्नाद्यं च किं पुनः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūryapyabhaktopāhṛtaṃ na me toṣāya kalpategandho dhūpaḥ sumanaso dīpo'nnādyaṃ ca kiṃ punaḥ
translation: What need be said if sandal-paste, incense, flowers, lamp, food, etc. are presented to me as offerings. On the contrary, even if rich gifts in abundance be offered to me, by a non-devotee, it does not contribute to my pleasure and joy.

devanagari: शुचिः सम्भृतसम्भारः प्राग्दर्भैः कल्पितासनः । आसीनः प्रागुदग् वार्चेद् अर्चायामथ सम्मुखः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: śuciḥ sambhṛtasambhāraḥ prāgdarbhaiḥ kalpitāsanaḥāsīnaḥ prāgudag vārced arcāyāmatha sammukhaḥ
translation: Purifying oneself (by taking a bath) and collecting together all the requisite articles of worship, seated on a seat of Darbha-grass with the Darbha’s pointing to the cast, one should face the East or the North or face the image of the god, if it was immovable.

devanagari: कृतन्यासः कृतन्यासां मदर्चां पाणिनामृजेत् । कलशं प्रोक्षणीयं च यथावदुपसाधयेत् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: kṛtanyāsaḥ kṛtanyāsāṃ madarcāṃ pāṇināmṛjetkalaśaṃ prokṣaṇīyaṃ ca yathāvadupasādhayet
translation:
Consecrating his person by Nyāsa (which consists of assignment of the various parts of the body to different deities accompanied with prayers and corresponding gesticulations) and the respective parts of the image, he should touch
it with his hand and clean it (by removing sandal-paste, flowers etc. of the previous worship) and should also duly consecrate the pitcher (kalaśa) containing water for bathing the deity and other vessels for sprinkling water.

devanagari: तदद्भिर्देवयजनं द्रव्याण्यात्मानमेव च । प्रोक्ष्य पात्राणि त्रीण्यद्भिः तैस्तैर्द्रव्यैश्च साधयेत् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tadadbhirdevayajanaṃ dravyāṇyātmānameva caprokṣya pātrāṇi trīṇyadbhiḥ taistairdravyaiśca sādhayet
translation: He should sprinkle that consecrated water from the Kalaśa) and sanctify the shrine of the deity, the articles of worship and one’s own person and also fill the three vessels with water and articles of worship[1].
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives the following details. The three vessels with water arc meant for washing the feet (pādya), hands (arghya) and for sipping (ācamanīya) water for the deity. The water for washing the feet of the deity should contain small grains of Śyāmāka rice, blades of Dūrvā grass, lotus flower and a herb called Viṣṇukrānta or Aparājita. The water for washing the hands of the deity should contain the following eight articles: sandalpaste, flowers, unbroken grains of rice (akṣata), of barley, blades of Kuśa grass, sesamum seeds, mustard seeds and blades of Dūrvā grass. The water for sipping or rinsing the deities’ mouth should contain nutmegs, cloves, berries called Kakkola or Kankola. The list is commonly given by all commentators with a few additions. Siddhāntapradīpa states that if any article is wanting- Tulasī leaves should be substituted.

devanagari: पाद्यार्घ्याचमनीयार्थं त्रीणि पात्राणि दैशिकः । हृदा शीर्ष्णाथ शिखया गायत्र्या चाभिमन्त्रयेत् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: pādyārghyācamanīyārthaṃ trīṇi pātrāṇi daiśikaḥhṛdā śīrṣṇātha śikhayā gāyatryā cābhimantrayet
translation: The worshipper should consecrate the three vessels containing water for washing the feet (pādya), hands (Arghya) and sipping (Ācamana) of the deity, with the Mantras[2] called Hṛdaya (heart), śīrṣan (head) and śikhā (Tuft of hair on the head) respectively, and then pronounce the Gāyatrī Mantra[3] over them all.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā. gives them as follows:
hṛdā namaḥ, sīrṣṇā svāhā, and śikhāyai vaṣaṭ.

Footnote [3]:
It is as follows:
tat-savitur vareṇyam bhargodevasya dhīmahi dhiyo yo naḥ pracodayā

devanagari: पिण्डे वाय्वग्निसंशुद्धे हृत्पद्मस्थां परां मम । अण्वीं जीवकलां ध्यायेत् नादान्ते सिद्धभाविताम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: piṇḍe vāyvagnisaṃśuddhe hṛtpadmasthāṃ parāṃ mamaaṇvīṃ jīvakalāṃ dhyāyet nādānte siddhabhāvitām
translation: One should contemplate on my transcendental atomic (subtle) aṃśa (part) of Paramātman which abides in the ‘Lotus’ of the heart in one’s body, which is dried up by the (gastric) wind, and parched up with fire at the Ādhāra cakra (a spiritual nerve-plexus presumed to be located at the navel), but revived by the sprinkling of nectar issuing from the moon located in the forehead. It is beyond the ‘un-struck’ (spiritual) sound (anāhata nāda) which is the fifth part of Praṇava (the remaining four being A, U, M, Bindu or nasal sound) and it is meditated upon by Siddhas (who have attained spiritual perfection).

devanagari: तयाऽत्मभूतया पिण्डे व्याप्ते सम्पूज्य तन्मयः । आवाह्यार्चादिषु स्थाप्य न्यस्ताङ्गं मां प्रपूजयेत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: tayā'tmabhūtayā piṇḍe vyāpte sampūjya tanmayaḥāvāhyārcādiṣu sthāpya nyastāṅgaṃ māṃ prapūjayet
translation: When his own body is permeated by that divine particle (aṃśa) or contemplated as his ātman (like a house illumined by the light emanated from a lamp, the worshipper should be (himself) full of my presence and invoke me and worship me with mentally created articles. He should then invoke my presence in the image (to be worshipped), perform the Nyāsa (assigning the various syllables of the Mantras to the part of its body) and then adore me that way.

devanagari: पाद्योपस्पर्शाहणादीन् उपचारान् प्रकल्पयेत् । धर्मादिभिश्च नवभिः कल्पयित्वाऽसनं मम ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: pādyopasparśāhaṇādīn upacārān prakalpayetdharmādibhiśca navabhiḥ kalpayitvā'sanaṃ mama
translation: After constructing mentally a throne[4] (seat) for me, and with the nine powers[5] attending upon it, he should conceive that there is (on the seat-cover designed over it) a shining lotus of eight petals beautified with a pericarp and filaments. With a view to secure success here and emancipation from saṃsāra hereafter, he should, according to Vedic and Tāntric procedure of worship, offer me water for washing feet (pādya), washing hands and rinsing the mouth.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives the following concept of this seat. It has Dharma or Righteousness, Jñāna or Knowledge, Vairāgya or Renunciation and Aiśvarya or Rulership as its legs in the South-east, South-west, North-west and Northeast corners and the opposites of the above viz. Unrighteousness, Ignorance, Attachment and lack of rulership as its four sides and the three guṇas—Sattva, Rajas and Tamas as the planks at the base.

Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā enumerates the following nine potencies of the Lord, Vimalā—purity, Utkarṣiṇī—exalted state, Jñāna—Knowledge, Kriyā—activity, Yoga—Yogic Supernormal powers, Prahvī—modesty, Satyā—truthfulness Īśānā—Sovereignty and Anugraha—Grace.

devanagari: पद्ममष्टदलं तत्र कर्णिकाकेसरोज्ज्वलम् । उभाभ्यां वेदतन्त्राभ्यां मह्यं तूभयसिद्धये ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: padmamaṣṭadalaṃ tatra karṇikākesarojjvalamubhābhyāṃ vedatantrābhyāṃ mahyaṃ tūbhayasiddhaye
translation: After constructing mentally a throne[4] (seat) for me, and with the nine powers[5] attending upon it, he should conceive that there is (on the seat-cover designed over it) a shining lotus of eight petals beautified with a pericarp and filaments. With a view to secure success here and emancipation from saṃsāra hereafter, he should, according to Vedic and Tāntric procedure of worship, offer me water for washing feet (pādya), washing hands and rinsing the mouth.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā gives the following concept of this seat. It has Dharma or Righteousness, Jñāna or Knowledge, Vairāgya or Renunciation and Aiśvarya or Rulership as its legs in the South-east, South-west, North-west and Northeast corners and the opposites of the above viz. Unrighteousness, Ignorance, Attachment and lack of rulership as its four sides and the three guṇas—Sattva, Rajas and Tamas as the planks at the base.

Footnote [5]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā enumerates the following nine potencies of the Lord, Vimalā—purity, Utkarṣiṇī—exalted state, Jñāna—Knowledge, Kriyā—activity, Yoga—Yogic Supernormal powers, Prahvī—modesty, Satyā—truthfulness Īśānā—Sovereignty and Anugraha—Grace.

devanagari: सुदर्शनं पाञ्चजन्यं गदासीषुधनुर्हलान् । मुसलं कौस्तुभं मालां श्रीवत्सं चानुपूजयेत् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: sudarśanaṃ pāñcajanyaṃ gadāsīṣudhanurhalānmusalaṃ kaustubhaṃ mālāṃ śrīvatsaṃ cānupūjayet
translation: He should duly worship in the serial order the discus the Sudarśana, the conch Pāñcajanya, the mace Kaumodakī, the sword Nandaka, the bow Śārṅga as well as the arms of Saṅkarṣaṇa, viz. the plough and the pestle, in the eight quarters and the Kaustubha gem, the Vaijayantī garland and the śrīvalsa mark (all the three on the chest of the Lord in their respective places).

devanagari: नन्दं सुनन्दं गरुडं प्रचण्डं चण्डमेव च । महाबलं बलं चैव कुमुदं कुमुदेक्षणम् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: nandaṃ sunandaṃ garuḍaṃ pracaṇḍaṃ caṇḍameva camahābalaṃ balaṃ caiva kumudaṃ kumudekṣaṇam
translation: Similarly, he should worship the eight attendants (Pārṣads) of the Lord, viz. Nanda, Sunanda, Pracaṇḍa, Caṇḍa as well as Mahābala, Bala, Kumuda and Kumudekṣaṇa, standing around the Lord in eight directions and Garuḍa facing Him in front.

devanagari: दुर्गां विनायकं व्यासं विष्वक्सेनं गुरून् सुरान् । स्वे स्वे स्थाने त्वभिमुखान् पूजयेत् प्रोक्षणादिभिः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: durgāṃ vināyakaṃ vyāsaṃ viṣvaksenaṃ gurūn surānsve sve sthāne tvabhimukhān pūjayet prokṣaṇādibhiḥ
translation: He should also adore by the usual offering of water to wash hand, etc., Durgā, Vināyaka, the sage Vyāsa and Vīṣvaksena [Viṣvaksena?] (all these surrounding the Lord in the four corners). His own spiritual guide (standing to the left of the God), and the guardian deities of the eight quarters, each in his respective position, all of them facing the Lord.

devanagari: चन्दनोशीरकर्पूर कुङ्कुमागुरुवासितैः । सलिलैः स्नापयेन्मन्त्रैः नित्यदा विभवे सति ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: candanośīrakarpūra kuṅkumāguruvāsitaiḥsalilaiḥ snāpayenmantraiḥ nityadā vibhave sati
translation: If he can. financially afford to do so, he should every day give me (and my attendants) bath with water fragrant with sandal paste, Uśīra (a fragrant root of the plant Andropogon muricatus), camphor, saffron and aloe-wood chanting all the while Mantras during the bath.

devanagari: स्वर्णघर्मानुवाकेन महापुरुषविद्यया । पौरुषेणापि सूक्तेन सामभी राजनादिभिः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: svarṇagharmānuvākena mahāpuruṣavidyayāpauruṣeṇāpi sūktena sāmabhī rājanādibhiḥ
translation: He should chant (while bathing the deity) the svarṇagharmānuvāka (the section of the Taittarīya Āraṇyaka beginning with the mantra: suvarṇaṃ gharmaṃ pariveda venam (T. A. 3. II.la) the Mahāpuruṣa Vidyā[6], the Puruṣa sūkta (Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. X.90) and texts of the Sāmaveda known as Rājan-sāma[7] (and Rauhiṇa sāma).
purport:
Footnote [6]:
It contains the following Mantra:
jitaṃ te puṇḍarīkākṣa namaste viśva-bhāvana /
subrahmaṇya namste'stu mahāpuruṣa-pūrvaja //

Footnote [7]:
It begins thus:
indraṃ naro nemadhitā havante
Ṛg Veda Saṃhitā. 7.27.1a, TS. 1.6.12.la, SV 1.318a
Padaratnāvalī includes the recitation of Viṣṇu Sūkta, Samudra Sūkta, Pavamāna Sūkta in addition to the above.

devanagari: वस्त्रोपवीताभरण पत्रस्रग्गन्धलेपनैः । अलङ्कुर्वीत सप्रेम मद्‍भक्तो मां यथोचितम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: vastropavītābharaṇa patrasraggandhalepanaiḥalaṅkurvīta saprema mad‍bhakto māṃ yathocitam
translation: My votary should lovingly decorate me appropriately with clothes, sacred thread, ornaments, decorative drawings on my body (on cheeks, chest, etc.), garlands of Tulasī leaves and sandal pigments.

devanagari: पाद्यं आचमनीयं च गन्धं सुमनसोऽक्षतान् । धूपदीपोपहार्याणि दद्यान्मे श्रद्धयार्चकः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: pādyaṃ ācamanīyaṃ ca gandhaṃ sumanaso'kṣatāndhūpadīpopahāryāṇi dadyānme śraddhayārcakaḥ
translation: My worshipper should offer to me with faith and reverence various articles (of worship) such as water for washing feet and rinsing the mouth (for sipping), sandal-paste, flowers, Akṣatas (grains of unbroken rice), scented fumes, light and articles of food.

devanagari: गुडपायससर्पींषि शष्कुल्यापूपमोदकान् । संयावदधिसूपांश्च नैवेद्यं सति कल्पयेत् ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: guḍapāyasasarpīṃṣi śaṣkulyāpūpamodakānsaṃyāvadadhisūpāṃśca naivedyaṃ sati kalpayet
translation: If he can afford it financially, sugar and milk preparations, ghee and butter, cakes) pastes and sweet-meats, porridge made of wheat flour with ghee and raw sugar, curds and soup of pulses should be offered to me.

devanagari: अभ्यङ्गोन्मर्दनादर्श दन्तधावाभिषेचनम् । अन्नाद्यगीतनृत्यादि पर्वणि स्युरुतान्वहम् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: abhyaṅgonmardanādarśa dantadhāvābhiṣecanamannādyagītanṛtyādi parvaṇi syurutānvaham
translation: Every day or (if that is not possible due to financial stringency) at least on holy days (like the eleventh day both in the bright and dark half of the month, etc.) or in the birthday celebrations of the incarnations of God, perfumed oil, paste of saffron and camphor (for massaging my body), a mirror (to show me my reflection), tooth brush for cleansing the teeth, bathing with pañcāmṛtas or five articles like milk, curds, ghee, honey and sugar, to be finally washed clean with, pure fresh water), sweet food for eating should be offered to me and music and dance should be presented on these days.

devanagari: विधिना विहिते कुण्डे मेखलागर्तवेदिभिः । अग्निमाधाय परितः समूहेत् पाणिनोदितम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vidhinā vihite kuṇḍe mekhalāgartavedibhiḥagnimādhāya paritaḥ samūhet pāṇinoditam
translation: (To those who wish a higher type of fruit, worship of sacrificial fire is recommended as. follows: A sacrificial receptacle sufficiently hollow in the middle prepared as per prescribed rules, in the earth along with three zone-like constructions and platforms around it be prepared and fire be deposited in it and when sufficiently kindled, he should collect it with his hand.

devanagari: परिस्तीर्याथ पर्युक्षेद् अन्वाधाय यथाविधि । प्रोक्षण्याऽसाद्य द्रव्याणि प्रोक्ष्याग्नौ भावयेत माम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: paristīryātha paryukṣed anvādhāya yathāvidhiprokṣaṇyā'sādya dravyāṇi prokṣyāgnau bhāvayeta mām
translation: After spreading the Kuśa grass on all sides (as per usual procedure), he should sprinkle water on all the four sides of the receptacle of fire and after performing the procedure of Anvādhāna (depositing fuel[8] and other things in the sacred fire), the worshipper should contemplate me in the fire, as being brilliant in complexion like red-hot molten gold, with four beautiful arms equipped with a conch, a discus, a mace and a lotus, gracious and serene in appearance, clad in raiments of golden colour like pollens of a lotus flower.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
The sacred fuel and articles to be offered arc to be placed to the north of the fire. They are to be sprinkled with consecrated water taken in a spoon before offering them to the fire.

devanagari: तप्तजाम्बूनदप्रख्यं शङ्खचक्रगदाम्बुजैः । लसच्चतुर्भुजं शान्तं पद्मकिञ्जल्कवाससम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: taptajāmbūnadaprakhyaṃ śaṅkhacakragadāmbujaiḥlasaccaturbhujaṃ śāntaṃ padmakiñjalkavāsasam
translation: After spreading the Kuśa grass on all sides (as per usual procedure), he should sprinkle water on all the four sides of the receptacle of fire and after performing the procedure of Anvādhāna (depositing fuel[8] and other things in the sacred fire), the worshipper should contemplate me in the fire, as being brilliant in complexion like red-hot molten gold, with four beautiful arms equipped with a conch, a discus, a mace and a lotus, gracious and serene in appearance, clad in raiments of golden colour like pollens of a lotus flower.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
The sacred fuel and articles to be offered arc to be placed to the north of the fire. They are to be sprinkled with consecrated water taken in a spoon before offering them to the fire.

devanagari: स्फुरत्किरीटकटक कटिसूत्रवराङ्गदम् । श्रीवत्सवक्षसं भ्राजत् कौस्तुभं वनमालिनम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: sphuratkirīṭakaṭaka kaṭisūtravarāṅgadamśrīvatsavakṣasaṃ bhrājat kaustubhaṃ vanamālinam
translation: (He should contemplate me as) adorned with a lustrous crown, with bracelets and a girdle and excellent armlets; with śrīvatsa mark on the chest, and shining Kaustubha gem (round the neck), and a garland of forest flowers (Vanamālā)

devanagari: ध्यायन्नभ्यर्च्य दारूणि हविषाभिघृतानि च । प्रास्याज्यभागावाघारौ दत्त्वा चाज्यप्लुतं हविः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: dhyāyannabhyarcya dārūṇi haviṣābhighṛtāni caprāsyājyabhāgāvāghārau dattvā cājyaplutaṃ haviḥ
translation: A wise person (knower of these technicalities) should pour the offerings into the fire, uttering the fundamental Mantra.[11] (Om namo Nārāyaṇāya) and some portion of the oblations with each of the sixteen verses of puruṣa sūkta. He should then give oblations (through the sacrificial fire) to Dharma and other gods according to the sequence given above (vide verse 25 above), uttering the name of the deity (in the Dative case and adding the word Svāhā every time. Finally, at the end of the sacrifice a thanks-giving oblation to fire with the Mantra Agnaye sviṣṭakṛte svāhā is to be offered.
purport:
Footnote [11]:
This being the Pāñcarātra Sādhanā, they won’t use the mantra Om Namo Vāsudevāya, though some modern commentators do so.

devanagari: अभ्यर्च्याथ नमस्कृत्य पार्षदेभ्यो बलिं हरेत् । मूलमन्त्रं जपेद् ब्रह्म स्मरन्नारायणात्मकम् ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: abhyarcyātha namaskṛtya pārṣadebhyo baliṃ haretmūlamantraṃ japed brahma smarannārāyaṇātmakam
translation: Having worshipped me and paying me obeisance, he should offer worship to the attendant deities (Nanda, Sunanda, etc.) muttering all the while the Mūla Mantra (Om namo Nārāyaṇāya) meditating on the Supreme Brahman, viz. Nārāyaṇa.

devanagari: दत्त्वाऽचमनमुच्छेषं विष्वक्सेनाय कल्पयेत् । मुखवासं सुरभिमत् ताम्बूलाद्यमथाहयेत् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: dattvā'camanamuccheṣaṃ viṣvaksenāya kalpayetmukhavāsaṃ surabhimat tāmbūlādyamathāhayet
translation: Having offered water for sipping, he should hand over the remnants to viṣvaksena. Then he should offer betel leaves containing spices and aromatic substances making the mouth sweetsmelling. He should conclude by mantra-puṣpa (showering flowers on me).

devanagari: उपगायन् गृणन् नृत्यन् कर्माणि अभिनयन् मम । मत्कथाः श्रावयन् श्रृण्वन् मुहूर्तं क्षणिको भवेत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: upagāyan gṛṇan nṛtyan karmāṇi abhinayan mamamatkathāḥ śrāvayan śrṛṇvan muhūrtaṃ kṣaṇiko bhavet
translation: After singing and extolling my excellences, dancing before me, imitating my past līlās (sportive actions), recounting and listening to my stories, he should remain immersed in that blissful state for a while.

devanagari: स्तवैरुच्चावचैः स्तोत्रैः पौराणैः प्राकृतैरपि । स्तुत्वा प्रसीद भगवन् इति वन्देत दण्डवत् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: stavairuccāvacaiḥ stotraiḥ paurāṇaiḥ prākṛtairapistutvā prasīda bhagavan iti vandeta daṇḍavat
translation: Eulogizing me by singing various hymns found in ancient texts as well as those in the current language (of the masses), he should lay himself prostrate before me with the prayer, “may you be propitious unto me, O Lord.

devanagari: शिरो मत्पादयोः कृत्वा बाहुभ्यां च परस्परम् । प्रपन्नं पाहि मामीश भीतं मृत्युग्रहार्णवात् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: śiro matpādayoḥ kṛtvā bāhubhyāṃ ca parasparamprapannaṃ pāhi māmīśa bhītaṃ mṛtyugrahārṇavāt
translation: Placing his head on my feet and clasping them (my feet) by both of his hands (he should pray) “protect me, O Lord. Afraid of the sea of saṃsāra full of alligators in the form of death, I have sought protection in you.”

devanagari: इति शेषां मया दत्तां शिरस्याधाय सादरम् । उद्वासयेद् चेद् उद्वास्यं ज्योतिर्ज्योतिषि तत्पुनः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: iti śeṣāṃ mayā dattāṃ śirasyādhāya sādaramudvāsayed ced udvāsyaṃ jyotirjyotiṣi tatpunaḥ
translation: He should then reverentially place on his head some flowers, etc. from my worship as a gracious gift from me. If withdrawing of the spiritual light invoked in the image is necessary, he should contemplate that the divine light in the image, is now withdrawn and merged in the Supreme Light in the lotus of his heart.

devanagari: अर्चादिषु यदा यत्र श्रद्धा मां तत्र चार्चयेत् । सर्वभूतेष्वात्मनि च सर्वात्माहं अवस्थितः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: arcādiṣu yadā yatra śraddhā māṃ tatra cārcayetsarvabhūteṣvātmani ca sarvātmāhaṃ avasthitaḥ
translation: One may worship me at any time and in any form (image, picture, etc.), in which one entertains devout faith. For being the Soul (Inner Controller) of all I abide in all beings as well as in the Self of the worshipper.

devanagari: एवं क्रियायोगपथैः पुमान्वैदिकतान्त्रिकैः । अर्चन् उभयतः सिद्धिं मत्तो विन्दत्यभीप्सिताम् ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kriyāyogapathaiḥ pumānvaidikatāntrikaiḥarcan ubhayataḥ siddhiṃ matto vindatyabhīpsitām
translation: He who worships in the manner described above, according to the course of active yoga as taught in the Vedas and the Tantras, receives from me accomplishments of his desired objectives, both here and hereafter.

devanagari: मदर्चां सम्प्रतिष्ठाप्य मन्दिरं कारयेद् दृढम् । पुष्पोद्यानानि रम्याणि पूजायात्रोत्सवाश्रितान् ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: madarcāṃ sampratiṣṭhāpya mandiraṃ kārayed dṛḍhampuṣpodyānāni ramyāṇi pūjāyātrotsavāśritān
translation: After installing my image, he should get a fine durable temple or shrine built for it; (he should get) beautiful flower-gardens laid out for it, and establish foundations (or endowments) for the regular performance of my daily worship as well as for celebration of festivals and for pilgrimage.

devanagari: पूजादीनां प्रवाहार्थं महापर्वस्वथान्वहम् । क्षेत्रापणपुरग्रामान् दत्त्वा मत्सार्ष्टितामियात् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: pūjādīnāṃ pravāhārthaṃ mahāparvasvathānvahamkṣetrāpaṇapuragrāmān dattvā matsārṣṭitāmiyāt
translation: He who makes formal grants of lands, markets, villages and towns for the perpetual continuation of my worship etc. every day as well as on specially sacred or festive occasions, attains to that divine state in which he can enjoy lordly blessings similar to mine.

devanagari: प्रतिष्ठया सार्वभौमं सद्मना भुवनत्रयम् । पूजादिना ब्रह्मलोकं त्रिभिर्मत्साम्यतामियात् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: pratiṣṭhayā sārvabhaumaṃ sadmanā bhuvanatrayampūjādinā brahmalokaṃ tribhirmatsāmyatāmiyāt
translation: By installing rṇy image, one attains the sovereignty of the entire globe (in future); by building a temple or a shrine, he is sure to get dominion over the three worlds; by performing worship and rendering other services, he can attain to the region of god Brahmā. But he who serves me in all the above-mentioned three ways, attains a state of (blissful) equality with me.

devanagari: मामेव नैरपेक्ष्येण भक्तियोगेन विन्दति । भक्तियोगं स लभते एवं यः पूजयेत माम् ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: māmeva nairapekṣyeṇa bhaktiyogena vindatibhaktiyogaṃ sa labhate evaṃ yaḥ pūjayeta mām
translation: A person who follows the path of disinterested devotion to me, directly attains to me only. He who worships’ me in the manner described above, is blessed with the Bhakti Yoga (the path of real devotion to me).

devanagari: यः स्वदत्तां परैर्दत्तां हरेत सुरविप्रयोः । वृत्तिं स जायते विड्भुग् वर्षाणां अयुतायुतम् ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: yaḥ svadattāṃ parairdattāṃ hareta suraviprayoḥvṛttiṃ sa jāyate viḍbhug varṣāṇāṃ ayutāyutam
translation: He who confiscates the endowment founded either by himself or by others for the sake of the deity or Brāhmaṇas, is destined to be born as a feces-eating worm for ten crores of years.

devanagari: कर्तुश्च सारथेहेतोः अनुमोदितुरेव च । कर्मणां भागिनः प्रेत्य भूयो भूयसि तत्फलम् ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: kartuśca sārathehetoḥ anumoditureva cakarmaṇāṃ bhāginaḥ pretya bhūyo bhūyasi tatphalam
translation:
Whatever fruit is destined for the actual agent of a deed, awaits also his colleague, his director or the appreciator of it, as all these are the collaborators in that act. And in the next world, everyone of them shares the reward or retribution in proportion to their contribution to that deed.
[back to top]
